heroCopy.headline: You raise the standard when conditions get loose.
heroCopy.subheadline: Precise, corrective, and unwilling to let weak execution pass unnoticed.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where accuracy, scrutiny, and directional force combine into a highly demanding presence.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to spot flaws quickly and respond by narrowing ambiguity, tightening expectations, and making quality visible. At your best this protects standards; overused, it can make the room feel more judged than helped.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure usually sharpens this pattern further, turning concern into corrective intensity and lower tolerance for loose thinking.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You are often strongest in contexts that genuinely need quality control, disciplined reasoning, and clear performance edges.

heroCopy.headline: You turn direction into visible execution pressure.
heroCopy.subheadline: Task-led, forceful, and inclined to push delivery before momentum fades.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles that combine a strong delivery bias with enough assertive drive to keep others moving.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to create progress by making ownership visible, raising the bar on follow-through, and keeping the work close to action. That can be highly effective when outcomes matter quickly, though it can feel abrupt if others need more consultation before they commit.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure often makes this pattern more direct, more outcome-focused, and less tolerant of drift.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually do best where delivery discipline and visible accountability are treated as core to performance.

heroCopy.headline: You keep people coordinated by being reliably present.
heroCopy.subheadline: Socially steady, quietly containing, and more interested in keeping the system workable than dramatic.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where people focus and stability combine into a dependable connective role.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to create cohesion by staying usable, reducing unnecessary churn, and helping others feel the work is still manageable. The strength is reliable connection; the risk is staying in support mode when the context needs a stronger pivot or firmer challenge.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure may increase your instinct to create steadiness for others and prevent the system from becoming fragmented.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You often do well in environments that value trust, continuity, and socially reliable coordination.

heroCopy.headline: You build confidence through care and precision.
heroCopy.subheadline: Methodical, quality-conscious, and more interested in robustness than rush.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles that slow the work down enough to improve quality, judgement, and long-term reliability.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to create value by checking sequence, improving logic, and making sure the work stands up after the first pass. Others may lean on you for rigour while also wishing you would sometimes move before every uncertainty has been reduced.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, you may become even more careful about evidence, sequence, and quality thresholds.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually fit best where strong standards, craft discipline, and thoughtful execution are respected rather than treated as drag.

heroCopy.headline: You keep progress alive by staying open and mobile.
heroCopy.subheadline: Socially agile and willing to adapt the route to keep people engaged.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where flexibility and people orientation combine more strongly than structure or force.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to make movement possible by adjusting approach, reading the room, and finding ways to keep others with the work even as conditions evolve. The strength is responsiveness; the risk is that constant adaptation can make your centre harder for others to read.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure may increase your tendency to keep options open and preserve room to manoeuvre rather than closing the system down too early.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You tend to work well in settings that reward collaboration, adaptation, and relationship-sensitive progress.

heroCopy.headline: You push momentum into motion quickly.
heroCopy.subheadline: Fast, assertive, and willing to make the path visible before the room fully settles.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles that combine speed with a strong instinct to take hold of direction.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to read hesitation as a signal to step in, sharpen priorities, and move the work forward decisively. At your best this creates traction quickly; overused, it can make others feel as though they are being pulled rather than brought along.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, this pattern usually becomes firmer, faster, and less patient with drift.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You are often strongest where urgency, ownership, and clear forward motion are genuinely valuable.

heroCopy.headline: You keep progress alive by staying open and mobile.
heroCopy.subheadline: Socially agile and willing to adapt the route to keep people engaged.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where flexibility and people orientation combine more strongly than structure or force.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to make movement possible by adjusting approach, reading the room, and finding ways to keep others with the work even as conditions evolve. The strength is responsiveness; the risk is that constant adaptation can make your centre harder for others to read.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure may increase your tendency to keep options open and preserve room to manoeuvre rather than closing the system down too early.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You tend to work well in settings that reward collaboration, adaptation, and relationship-sensitive progress.

heroCopy.headline: You create steadiness through thoughtful sequencing.
heroCopy.subheadline: Measured, dependable, and inclined to make the work safer by planning it properly.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where deliberation and stability rise together into a calm planning centre.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to protect outcomes by thinking ahead, reducing avoidable volatility, and building enough certainty for others to trust the route. The strength is dependable judgement; the risk is holding the system in planning mode longer than the moment requires.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, you may double down on sequence, pacing, and reducing avoidable disruption.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually fit best where continuity, preparation, and considered delivery are all taken seriously.

heroCopy.headline: You turn direction into visible execution pressure.
heroCopy.subheadline: Task-led, forceful, and inclined to push delivery before momentum fades.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles that combine a strong delivery bias with enough assertive drive to keep others moving.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to create progress by making ownership visible, raising the bar on follow-through, and keeping the work close to action. That can be highly effective when outcomes matter quickly, though it can feel abrupt if others need more consultation before they commit.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure often makes this pattern more direct, more outcome-focused, and less tolerant of drift.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually do best where delivery discipline and visible accountability are treated as core to performance.

heroCopy.headline: You move people by creating energy around the work.
heroCopy.subheadline: Visible, connective, and able to create momentum through engagement rather than force alone.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where people focus and pace rise together strongly enough to make your impact feel social as well as directional.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to build progress by keeping people engaged, making the work feel alive, and pulling others into movement early. The benefit is momentum with connection; the risk is that pace and positivity can outrun the harder edges of detail or follow-through.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, you may work even harder to keep others connected, motivated, and moving rather than letting the room flatten out.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually do best where visibility, collaboration, and momentum-building are valued as real levers of performance.

heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure usually sharpens this pattern further, turning concern into corrective intensity and lower tolerance for loose thinking.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You are often strongest in contexts that genuinely need quality control, disciplined reasoning, and clear performance edges.

heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, you may work even harder to keep others connected, motivated, and moving rather than letting the room flatten out.

heroCopy.headline: You present as broadly balanced rather than sharply polarised.
heroCopy.subheadline: No stronger Hero rule dominates, so the profile resolves to a steady central pattern.
heroCopy.summary: This fallback fits profiles that spread across several traits without crossing a more specific rule cleanly.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to show usable range across contexts, with behaviour shaped more by the situation than by one consistently dominant Hero signature. That can be an advantage in varied roles, though it also means the profile may need richer supporting sections to feel fully distinctive.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, the profile may borrow from several nearby styles rather than defaulting to one obvious response.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually fit best in environments that value versatility, role balance, and context-sensitive judgement.

heroCopy.headline: You move people by creating energy around the work.
heroCopy.subheadline: Visible, connective, and able to create momentum through engagement rather than force alone.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where people focus and pace rise together strongly enough to make your impact feel social as well as directional.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to build progress by keeping people engaged, making the work feel alive, and pulling others into movement early. The benefit is momentum with connection; the risk is that pace and positivity can outrun the harder edges of detail or follow-through.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, you may work even harder to keep others connected, motivated, and moving rather than letting the room flatten out.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually do best where visibility, collaboration, and momentum-building are valued as real levers of performance.

heroCopy.subheadline: Fast, assertive, and willing to make the path visible before the room fully settles.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles that combine speed with a strong instinct to take hold of direction.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to read hesitation as a signal to step in, sharpen priorities, and move the work forward decisively. At your best this creates traction quickly; overused, it can make others feel as though they are being pulled rather than brought along.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, this pattern usually becomes firmer, faster, and less patient with drift.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You are often strongest where urgency, ownership, and clear forward motion are genuinely valuable.

heroCopy.headline: You bring structure to change without losing initiative.
heroCopy.subheadline: Adaptive but organised, with enough force to keep movement coherent.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles that combine changing-course ability with visible structure and direction.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to stabilise moving situations by creating enough order for action to stay coordinated without making the system rigid. The advantage is agile control; the risk is expecting others to keep up with a plan that is still evolving as you drive it.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure usually sharpens your need to coordinate adaptation rather than leaving the change process loose.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You often do well where teams need both responsiveness and clearer operating discipline at the same time.

heroCopy.headline: You turn direction into visible execution pressure.
heroCopy.subheadline: Task-led, forceful, and inclined to push delivery before momentum fades.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles that combine a strong delivery bias with enough assertive drive to keep others moving.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to create progress by making ownership visible, raising the bar on follow-through, and keeping the work close to action. That can be highly effective when outcomes matter quickly, though it can feel abrupt if others need more consultation before they commit.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure often makes this pattern more direct, more outcome-focused, and less tolerant of drift.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually do best where delivery discipline and visible accountability are treated as core to performance.

heroCopy.headline: You create confidence through steady delivery.
heroCopy.subheadline: Reliable, task-led, and inclined to keep the work moving without unnecessary noise.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where task ownership and steadiness rise together into a delivery-first presence.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to add value by making progress dependable, keeping commitments visible, and reducing the chances that momentum breaks down through inconsistency. That can make you highly trusted, though others may sometimes want more visible stretch or experimentation from you.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, you often narrow toward reliable follow-through and protecting what still needs to get done.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually fit best where dependable execution and low-drama ownership are more useful than constant reinvention.

heroCopy.headline: You create pace that still keeps people connected.
heroCopy.subheadline: Fast-moving and socially aware, with momentum built through inclusion rather than force alone.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where pace and people focus rise together strongly enough to feel both energising and connective.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to move the work forward while still keeping others in the picture, making urgency feel shared rather than purely imposed. The advantage is energetic coordination; the risk is expecting momentum itself to solve problems that still need sharper structure.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Under pressure, you may push for movement while working hard to keep alignment and morale from collapsing.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You tend to work well where high momentum still depends on people staying connected to the direction.

heroCopy.headline: You keep progress alive by staying open and mobile.
heroCopy.subheadline: Socially agile and willing to adapt the route to keep people engaged.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where flexibility and people orientation combine more strongly than structure or force.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to make movement possible by adjusting approach, reading the room, and finding ways to keep others with the work even as conditions evolve. The strength is responsiveness; the risk is that constant adaptation can make your centre harder for others to read.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure may increase your tendency to keep options open and preserve room to manoeuvre rather than closing the system down too early.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You tend to work well in settings that reward collaboration, adaptation, and relationship-sensitive progress.

heroCopy.headline: You hold people and standards together steadily.
heroCopy.subheadline: Relationally aware, deliberate, and protective of continuity rather than drama.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles where people care, steadiness, and careful judgement combine into a stewardship style.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to create trust by being thoughtful, dependable, and socially aware without becoming reactive. That gives you a strong holding quality, though others may sometimes need a clearer signal about when you will stop containing and start deciding.
heroCopy.pressureOverlay: Pressure often strengthens your instinct to stay composed, protect continuity, and keep others anchored.
heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You often work well where calm stewardship, trust, and measured judgement matter more than theatrical leadership.

heroCopy.headline: You build confidence through care and precision.
heroCopy.subheadline: Methodical, quality-conscious, and more interested in robustness than rush.
heroCopy.summary: This pattern fits profiles that slow the work down enough to improve quality, judgement, and long-term reliability.
heroCopy.narrative: You are likely to create value by checking sequence, improving logic, and making sure the work stands up after the first pass. Others may lean on you for rigour while also wishing you would sometimes move before every uncertainty has been reduced.

heroCopy.environmentOverlay: You usually fit best where strong standards, craft discipline, and thoughtful execution are respected rather than treated as drag.

rules: adaptive_catalyst: tightened with a small exclusion set against strongly social, structured, and highly assertive profiles so it keeps the adaptive-flex lane.
adaptive_challenger: left active but lightly tightened against strongly social neighbours to keep the force/pace lane clear.
adaptive_orchestrator: kept active as the adaptive-structured lane, but now excludes strongly social, strongly assertive, and strongly stable profiles to reduce deep overlap stacks.
balanced_operator: unchanged fallback retained as the final deterministic catch-all.
calm_operator: retired from the active refined set; its space is covered by steady_executor and grounded_planner.
decisive_orchestrator: broadened slightly by lowering structured to 3 so more mid-range execution-control profiles avoid fallback.
deliberate_craftsperson: left broadly available as the structured craft lane after stable exclusions proved too costly to coverage.
delivery_commander: left in place as the task-led assertive lane; stable exclusion was removed after over-cutting strong delivery profiles.
diplomatic_stabiliser: kept in the receptive-stable lane, but narrowed away from connector, mediator, and grounded stewardship cases.
driving_integrator: kept active for high paced + people-led profiles, with only higher-flex and higher-structured exclusions to reduce the worst overlap families.
exacting_controller: unchanged from round 2 because it was already clean, high-confidence, and low-collision.
flexible_mobiliser: narrowed with a higher pace exclusion so it captures lower-pace flexible social profiles while giving faster social profiles to pace-led neighbours.
forceful_driver: left broad so high paced + assertive profiles remain reachable even when they also carry moderate social range.
grounded_planner: left active with only a stronger people-led exclusion after narrower variants over-cut grounded mid-range coverage.
grounded_steward: left active but excludes receptive-heavy cases so it stays distinct from diplomacy.
relational_catalyst: left active but excludes only the highest-flex and highest-pace cases so it reduces overlap without collapsing too much social-pace coverage.
responsive_mediator: retained as the receptive+tolerant+people mediation lane, with only a higher-stability exclusion to stay out of connector territory.
steady_connector: narrowed to the social-stable lane with a higher deliberate exclusion so grounded stewardship can keep its own lane.
steady_executor: still narrowed to the task-stable lane, but stable was lowered to 2 to capture more delivery-grounded mid-range profiles.
structured_collaborator: narrowed with a higher adaptive exclusion so it keeps the structured-social lane without overlapping adaptive_orchestrator as often.
pairTraitWeights: No pair-trait weight mappings changed in round 3; refinement was achieved through rule separation, threshold tuning, and minimal exclusions.
patterns: retained and redefined: adaptive_catalyst, adaptive_challenger, adaptive_orchestrator, decisive_orchestrator, deliberate_craftsperson, delivery_commander, diplomatic_stabiliser, driving_integrator, exacting_controller, flexible_mobiliser, forceful_driver, grounded_planner, grounded_steward, relational_catalyst, responsive_mediator, steady_connector, steady_executor, structured_collaborator
